structData.title: Pixel fires but conversions not attributed — ITP and cookie match rate issues
structData.content:
If your conversion pixel is firing (you can see fire events in getPixelFireLog) but the conversions are not being attributed to your campaigns, the most common cause is a low cookie match rate caused by browser privacy restrictions, particularly Apple ITP (Intelligent Tracking Prevention).

HOW TO DIAGNOSE
Call getConversionMatchRate and check:
  cookieMatchRate   — if below 20%, ITP is severely impacting attribution
  safariFraction    — if above 40% of your users are on Safari, ITP is the primary cause
  itpImpact: HIGH   — confirms ITP is the problem

Call getAttributionWindowSettings and check:
  clickThroughWindowDays — if set to 30, this is longer than ITP's 7-day cookie limit

WHAT ITP DOES
Apple ITP (present in Safari on iPhone, iPad, and Mac) limits third-party cookies to 7 days in most cases and blocks them entirely in some contexts (cross-site tracking). Since programmatic attribution relies on matching a third-party cookie set at ad impression time to the same cookie at conversion time, any user who converts more than 7 days after seeing your ad will not be attributed — even if they clicked the ad.

MATCH RATE THRESHOLDS
  Above 60%: Healthy — ITP impact is minimal
  40%–60%:   Moderate impact — consider server-side tracking
  20%–40%:   High impact — implement fixes urgently
  Below 20%: Critical — majority of conversions are unattributed

FIXES IN ORDER OF EFFECTIVENESS
1. Server-side event tracking: Implement conversion tracking via your own first-party domain server. The pixel fires a server-to-server call that does not rely on third-party cookies. This eliminates ITP impact entirely.

2. First-party pixel domain (CNAME): Set up a CNAME record on your own domain (e.g. pixel.yourbrand.com) pointing to the DSP pixel endpoint. Safari treats this as a first-party cookie, extending the window. Note: as of ITP 2.3+, CNAME cloaking is also restricted — effectiveness varies.

3. Shorten attribution window: Change clickThroughWindowDays from 30 to 7 to match ITP's actual limit. This reduces overcounting of long-latency conversions and improves data accuracy.

4. Identity solutions: Migrate to ITP-resilient identity like UID2 (email-based) or RampID. These do not rely on third-party cookies and provide persistent user matching across sessions.
